Very useful po E-bike/ E-trike most especially sa mga senior citizens dito sa lugar namin.

I remember ADB promoting e trikes ages ago. Every DOE secretary that came wanted to put a pin on it.  Only a few seemed interested like boracay which felt it was a good alternative for the short trips. Nowadays, I see some senior highs driving to school on etrikes. A mom driving kids or going to the market.  But since they dont require drivers ed, there is a lack of discipline and a question of what rules do apply.

It is with great sadness that we do not possess the art and science of governance.

The proliferation of cars either combustion or EV powered, motorcycles, EV trikes came about due to one main issue. 

Transportation .

The need to move people or goods from one point to another could not be provided by the government. Thus, the need to satisfy immediately such sentiments became necessary. 

Unfortunately, like most government initiated programs, the lack of a comprehensive study on its  negative effects are studied ones we have experienced it. 

The liberalization of the importation of electric vehicles immediately solved the transport issue. However, like hydra, it created a lot of issues, such as,

1. Accidents
     How can we penalize an e trike drivers if they are not regulated since they are not required to have a license?
Who will pay for the damages since they are not insured?

2. Additional reckless drivers
We have witnessed accidents involving reckless or kamote drivers involved in preventable situations. Here we are, adding additional road accidents.

Further, I have seen very young and senior citizens weaving in and out of their lanes without any turning signs. Another entitled sector.

3. Infrastructure 
Prior to these e-trike and motorcycle era, we have already experienced lack of roads. Instead of addressing such issue, they even reduced a car’ mobility by reducing the roads by allocating a special lane for bikes. By the way, this is a correct program but wrong implementation. A 3 lane road becomes a 2 lane road. The 3rd lane is spliced into 2. One for bikes and the other one becomes non usable since a car can’t decently use it unless he do a James Bond, by driving only two wheels, in front and back while the other wheels are suspended in mid air.

Unfortunately, we have a penchant for solving an immediate need and let the next administration address any consequential problems and blame them for bad governance.

Our political leaders have a myopic view of governance wherein it is so focused on their present administration wherein accolades and praises should be confined while they are in power. 

Can the government still curtail the excesses of the e- trike industry? 

The question is, can we still return the squeezed toothpaste back to its tube?

I don't have a problem with e-bikes, e-trikes, and the 4-wheeled ones that look like golf carts. The problem is the one driving it. Kahit dun na lang sila maging firm, yung kailangan ng license para walang excuse na hindi nila alam na bawal or na ganun pala dapat. Kasi mga minors na driver, pati mga matanda na, pasingit-singit sa mga sasakyan, mga ang bibilis magpatakbo kahit may nakitang tatawid, mga sumasampa sa sidewalk tapos magagalit kasi ang bagal nyo maglakad hindi sya makadaan, saka yung nasa gitna sya ng daan pero mabagal andar nya at ayaw gumilid kahit bumubusina ka na kaya may parang prusisyon ng mga kotse sa likod nya. Kahit bigyan na lang ng discount sa pagkuha ng license pag e-bike etc. ang hawak, basta para sa akin dapat may license pag may motor at ginagamit sa public roads along with other vehicles.

Be ware of sharing, mentioning them, etc.

Look up the Publicus survey prior to the 2025 election.  Bam and Kiko placed high, and that corresponded with the actual election results.

What does that tell us when other surveys unanimously placed Bam and Kiko at the bottom or outside the top 12?  The other surveys had flawed methods, or worse, deliberately biased results.  What is even possible is that all these survey agencies have been deliberately falsifying survey results to fit one common client's wishes - only to falter when that client split in two.

I have a long standing, unproven theory that the survey firms were all hired to project the same results to the public, in order to make plausible rigged election results.  This was most likely the case in 2022 - not that they needed to for the win, but just to be sure because they were not leaving anything to chance.

The break up of the Uniteam was an opportunity to prove the point, as competing pay masters demand different survey results, the unanimous surveys pre 2022 election will be broken up.  That is exactly what happened in the 2025 election surveys, Publicus stood outside while everyone else stuck to the plan (the admins).

Does that make Publicus a truth telling, accurate agency?  We were not born yesterday.  Believe at your own risk.

"Beware that, when fighting monsters, you yourself do not become a monster.... for when you gaze long into the abyss, the abyss gazes also into you." 

Those words from the philosopher Nietzsche are not about ghosts or demons. They are about a danger each of us can recognize. It is about the peril of the fight. When we confront a great darkness, such as injustice or cruelty and suffering, we risk being consumed by it. We can become the very thing we sought to defeat.

Consider the story of Harry Roque. Here was a man who once stood with the victims. As a human rights lawyer, he gazed into the abyss. He looked directly at the suffering of the powerless and the ravages of impunity.

He gave voice to the victims of massacre. He comforted women seeking justice. He knew the language of international law. He saw the ICC as a tool for righteousness, a light to illuminate darkness.

But then he joined the administration of Rodrigo Duterte. He became the public voice for a war on drugs that created thousands more victims. The advocate who once used his voice to condemn injustice then used it to explain injustice away. He did not just gaze into the abyss. He stepped into its service.

Now history turns. Duterte is to be brought before the very court Roque once lauded. The light of the ICC is finally aimed at the darkness.

So we must ask what this moment demands of Harry Roque. 

The easy verdict is to say he is a hypocrite, a sellout. But the harder and more important question is this: What do we owe to principles after we have betrayed them?

That question is whether someone who justified the abyss can rediscover the moral clarity that once condemned it. The light of justice can expose the facts, but only an examination of one's own conscience can illuminate the darkness within.

The court will judge Duterte. But for Roque, for the supporters of Duterte, and for all of us watching, the harder judgment is the one we make upon ourselves. It is the judgment we face when we finally look away from the abyss and ask ourselves what we have become.

Can you turn away from the abyss once it has taken up residence in your soul?
